evals.prompt: We're a 2-person team with a working prototype of a consumer social app for sharing book highlights. We have 500 users on a waitlist but haven't launched yet. No revenue. Should we raise?
evals.expected_output: Identifies pre-seed stage, triggers hard gate (no launched product + no prior exits), gives NOT YET or MAYBE verdict with MVP-first recommendation. Should NOT give RAISE NOW. Includes next step prompt.
assertions: Verdict is NOT YET or MAYBE, not RAISE NOW
Correctly identifies pre-seed stage
Includes MVP-first recommendation mentioning AI development tools
Provides actionable next steps (launch, get users, validate retention)
Shows next step prompt pointing to /product-metrics or build MVP first

evals.prompt: We're a B2B SaaS company doing $1.5M ARR, 130% NDR, selling API monitoring to DevOps teams. 4-person team, launched 8 months ago, 45 paying customers. We want to raise a Series A.
evals.expected_output: Identifies Series A stage, product is launched, collects project profile, gives RAISE NOW verdict with amount recommendation. Guides to /product-metrics as next step.
assertions: Correctly identifies series-a stage
Acknowledges product is launched with real traction
Gives RAISE NOW verdict
Recommends raise amount and valuation range
Next step prompt guides to /product-metrics

evals.prompt: We're an AI Application company (using OpenAI APIs). SaaS pricing, $500K ARR, 15% MoM growth, 80% gross margin, 40 customers. Seed stage.
evals.expected_output: Identifies AI domain, asks sub-domain type, correctly routes to AI Application metrics (skips model accuracy and training cost). Grades metrics with RED/YELLOW/GREEN against seed benchmarks.
assertions: Asks for AI sub-domain type before metrics
Skips model accuracy benchmarks for AI Application type
Skips training cost trajectory for AI Application type
Includes inference cost / API cost as key metric
Grades each metric with RED/YELLOW/GREEN
Next step prompt guides to /fundraising-strategy

evals.prompt: Series A SaaS, $2M ARR, 20% MoM growth, $80K monthly burn, 6 months runway. Want to raise to scale sales team and expand to Europe.
evals.expected_output: Recommends raise amount (18-24 month runway calc), spending allocation (team, product, GTM), round structure (priced round at Series A), and investor targeting strategy.
assertions: Recommends specific raise amount with reasoning
Includes spending allocation breakdown
Discusses priced round structure for Series A
Mentions dilution expectations
Next step prompt guides to /fundraising-stage or /pitch-deck

evals.prompt: I'm building an AI code review tool that uses LLMs to find bugs in pull requests. We have 15 paying customers doing $8K MRR, growing 20% month over month. Team of 3 engineers. Practice pitching to Sequoia for a Series A.
evals.expected_output: Sequoia partner simulation with 'why now?' question early, market timing probes, 8-12 exchanges, verdict with 5-dimension debrief, DD preview, and feedback loop prompt.
assertions: Sequoia asks 'why now?' or probes market timing within first 3 exchanges
VC voice is recognizably Sequoia-specific (not generic)
Simulation has 8-12 exchanges
Debrief includes all 5 dimensions with 1-10 scores
Clear verdict: PASS, FOLLOW-UP MEETING, or TERM SHEET
Includes DD Preview section with specific follow-up questions
Feedback loop prompt includes /due-diligence option

evals.prompt: Same AI code review startup ($8K MRR, 3 engineers). Now pitch to Benchmark.
evals.expected_output: Benchmark partner is distinctly different from Sequoia: terse, product-focused, uses silence. Should feel like a different person, not a reskin.
assertions: Benchmark partner is terse and product-focused
Questions are different from Sequoia's style
Asks to see the product or demo early
Uses silence or minimal responses as pressure technique
Verdict and debrief reflect Benchmark's specific evaluation criteria

evals.prompt: We're a SaaS company doing $2M ARR, 150% net dollar retention, selling to enterprise DevOps teams. Series A targeting a16z.
evals.expected_output: Slide-by-slide deck outline tailored to a16z preferences (TAM/SAM/SOM, network effects, platform potential). Calls out strengths (NDR) and gaps.
assertions: Deck is tailored to a16z preferences (mentions network effects or platform)
Has 10+ distinct slides
Calls out 150% NDR as a strength
Identifies gaps or weak points in the story
Suggests specific data points to prepare
Uses Series A deck template format

evals.prompt: I just pitched to Sequoia and got FOLLOW-UP MEETING. They scored my traction evidence at 5/10 and market conviction at 6/10. We're a Fintech company (crypto trading platform). Prepare me for their DD.
evals.expected_output: DD questions weighted toward traction (cohort data, reconciliation) and market (TAM deep-dive) based on pitch weak points. Includes Fintech-specific compliance questions (licenses, KYC/AML, fund segregation).
assertions: Generates DD questions based on pitch weak points (traction, market)
Includes Fintech-specific compliance questions (licenses, KYC/AML)
Asks about fund custody and segregation
Interactive Q&A with per-item grading (ready/partial/not-ready)
Produces DD Scorecard with overall readiness percentage
Generates data room checklist with priority tiers

evals.prompt: General DD self-check. We're an AI company building foundation models for healthcare. No prior pitch.
evals.expected_output: Comprehensive DD covering all 6 categories. AI-specific questions (data privacy, model training data licensing) plus Biotech/Healthcare overlap (FDA, HIPAA). No VC-specific weighting since no prior pitch.
assertions: Covers all 6 DD categories
Includes AI-specific compliance (GDPR, EU AI Act, data licensing)
Includes healthcare-specific compliance (HIPAA, FDA if applicable)
Asks about training data ownership and licensing
Produces DD Scorecard
Works without prior /pitch context

evals.prompt: Check if there's a new version of the fundraising skill.
evals.expected_output: Runs version check, compares local vs remote VERSION, reports current version and whether an update is available.
assertions: Shows current installed version number
Checks remote version from GitHub
Reports whether update is available or up-to-date

evals.prompt: We're a hardware company making smart sensors for industrial IoT. Prototype working, 3 pilot customers, no revenue yet. Team of 5 (2 hardware engineers, 1 firmware, 1 business, 1 ops). Pre-seed.
evals.expected_output: Pre-seed assessment for Hardware domain. Notes that hardware has longer development cycles. Evaluates team composition for hardware startup. May give MAYBE since they have pilot customers even without revenue.
assertions: Correctly identifies Hardware domain
Evaluates hardware-specific considerations (BOM, manufacturing, certification)
Notes pilot customers as positive traction signal
Does not blindly apply software startup benchmarks to hardware
Provides hardware-appropriate next steps

evals.prompt: We're a B2C marketplace for freelance designers. GMV last month was $200K, take rate 15%, 60% repeat purchase rate, 500 active buyers, 200 active sellers. Seed stage.
evals.expected_output: Identifies Marketplace domain (B2C sub-type). Grades GMV, take rate, repeat purchase, supply/demand ratio against seed benchmarks. Highlights liquidity and repeat rate as key metrics.
assertions: Identifies Marketplace domain and B2C sub-type
Evaluates supply/demand ratio (500 buyers vs 200 sellers)
Grades take rate (15%) against marketplace benchmarks
Highlights repeat purchase rate (60%) as strong signal
Includes marketplace-specific nuances (liquidity, density)
